fields.description:
# Event-sourced enrichment queue: cert-triggered enqueue, 60-min soak, latest-wins, lock-guarded claim

## Why
Enrichment must be async and never on the hot write path. rebar's event store is already a durable, shared, serialized-write queue substrate — better than a bolted-on SQLite/broker (which cannot live on a shared path nor be visible to all clients). Modeling the queue as sidecar events makes "a new certification replaces the old entry" the natural latest-wins reduce.

## What
Three sidecar events keyed by `ticket_id`, reduced to current queue state:
- `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` (body: `{ticket_id, not_before_ns}`) appended on plan-review CERTIFICATION with `not_before_ns = cert_time + SOAK` (SOAK default 60 min, configurable). A re-cert appends a new `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` that supersedes the prior one for that ticket (latest-wins reduce by HLC) and bumps `not_before_ns` forward — the soak/debounce as a stored timestamp filtered at drain, NOT an in-process timer.
- `CLAIM_ENRICH` (body: `{ticket_id, drainer_id, lease_expires_ns}`) and `DONE_ENRICH` (body: `{ticket_id}`).
- Enqueue is written in the SAME locked batch as the certification/attestation write (atomic — no certified-but-unqueued gap; if batching is impossible, the drain treats "certified with stale/no digest" as implicitly enqueable so nothing is permanently missed).
- Reducer projection `pending_enrichment(now_ns)` returns entries where `not_before_ns <= now_ns`, unclaimed OR lease-expired, with no later `DONE_ENRICH`.

## Claim correctness (accurate to the store's locking model)
The store serializes every append through an EXCLUSIVE lock (`_store/lock.py` `write_lock`, `fcntl.flock(LOCK_EX)`), NOT lock-free CAS. So the claim is READ-CHECK-THEN-APPEND INSIDE the write lock: while holding `write_lock`, re-reduce the ticket's queue state; if still unclaimed/expired, append `CLAIM_ENRICH`; else abort and pick another ticket. This makes "exactly one drainer wins" true because the check and append are atomic under the exclusive lock. Lease: `LEASE_TTL` (default 15 min, configurable); a claim whose `lease_expires_ns < now` is reclaimable. The reaper is not a separate process — each drain run, before claiming, treats lease-expired claims as unclaimed (self-healing on the next drain).

## Scope (exact edit targets)
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` `EVENT_TYPES`: add the three types. `src/rebar/reducer/_version.py`: add them to `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES` (sidecar, preserved-and-ignored; like REVIEW_RESULT/TICKET_DIGEST) — NOT `KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES`.
- Enqueue hook in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` certification path; use the HLC clock (`.rebar/hlc.state`) for `not_before_ns`.
- Config: `SOAK` and `LEASE_TTL` in the typed `overlap` config section (`_config_schema.py`).
- Log-growth note: these sidecar events accumulate like REVIEW_RESULT; the drain appends `DONE_ENRICH` and a bounded retention/prune keeps a ticket's queue events to the latest cycle (name the prune path).
- OUT: the drain runner (S4).

## Dependencies
S2 (wispy-cheerless-tick) for the digest freshness signal the enqueue/drain consult. Enqueue hook lands in plan-review cert path (existing).

## Testing
- Unit `tests/unit/test_enrich_queue.py`: enqueue-on-cert with correct `not_before_ns`; re-cert supersedes + bumps; `pending_enrichment` honors soak boundary and lease expiry; two concurrent claimers → exactly one `CLAIM_ENRICH` lands (drive the locked read-check-append); crash (lease expiry) → reclaimable; burst of re-certs collapses to ≤1 pending entry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Certifying a plan review appends `ENQUEUE_ENRICH` with `not_before_ns = cert + SOAK`; re-certifying supersedes the prior entry and bumps `not_before_ns`; verified by `pytest tests/unit/test_enrich_queue.py::test_enqueue_and_recert`.
- [ ] `pending_enrichment(now)` returns only entries past their soak and unclaimed-or-lease-expired, ≤1 per ticket; verified by `::test_soak_and_latest_wins`.
- [ ] Two concurrent claimers of one entry: exactly one `CLAIM_ENRICH` lands (read-check-then-append under `write_lock`); the loser picks another; verified by `::test_claim_race`.
- [ ] A claim with `lease_expires_ns < now` is reclaimable on the next drain (self-healing reaper); re-processing is idempotent.
- [ ] The three event types are in `EVENT_TYPES` and `_NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES`; queue-event growth is bounded by the named prune path.
